failedAssertions.reason: Step execution fails with 'Unknown instruction: MAIN'. All tested presets contain 'MAIN PROC' label in .CODE segment. The fix (commit 55bed82) correctly skips .DATA segment definitions but does not handle label definitions. Parser treats 'MAIN' as mnemonic and 'PROC' as operand.
Run/Halt buttons UI work correctly, but execution immediately fails due to P5-SIM-004 bug.

blockedAssertions.blockedBy: P5-SIM-004 failure - cannot test breakpoints when single-step execution is broken. No breakpoint UI controls found.
P5-SIM-004 failure - cannot verify instruction highlighting when stepping fails.

rootCause: Simulator parser correctly skips .DATA segment definitions (msg DB 'Hello') after commit 55bed82 fix, but does not handle label definitions like 'MAIN PROC' in .CODE segment. Labels followed by directives (PROC, ENDP, SEGMENT, ENDS, EQU) need to be skipped as well.
quickFixSuggestion: Parser needs additional logic to detect labels followed by directives (PROC, ENDP, SEGMENT, ENDS, EQU) and skip the entire line. Example: 'MAIN PROC' should be skipped, not parsed as instruction 'MAIN' with operand 'PROC'.
